steps.title: Find the needed pathway
steps.content: The team concluded that BovB moved from snakes to frogs through horizontal transfer, either directly or indirectly. Because frogs usually do not survive encounters with snakes, strong support would identify an indirect carrier that contacts both animals.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant findings
steps.content: A explains possible frog-to-frog transfer, not how frogs acquired the gene from snakes. C rules out one indirect bacterial route, which weakens the idea. D concerns fitness advantages, not transmission. The remaining finding supplies a parasite vector between snakes and frogs. The answer is B.
